purpose: Draft (inactive) question objects derived from the Vinci 2026-07-08 quiz-3 batch -- a completed, handwritten 'Review Quiz #3' (Water Survival, SHLSOS Course #549), 14 fill-in-the-blank questions across 3 pages. These are NOT part of src/content/course.json's live "questions" array and will not appear in the app until explicitly promoted.
activationRule: Do not add any of these questions to course.json unless its verificationStatus has been changed away from 'draft-unverified'. q-quiz3-lifeboat4-location-scenario and q-quiz3-stationbill-itemslist are recommended needs-review (not quiz-derived-study) -- see each question's sourceReference for why.
duplicateCheckNotes: Cross-referenced against all 228 live questions in src/content/course.json before drafting -- see the fuller duplicateCheckNotes in vinci-2026-07-08-quiz-3-source-intake.json for the specific matches found. Four of the 14 source questions were deliberately not drafted at all (exact/near-exact duplicates of live content, no new angle): Q1 (bucket as sea-anchor substitute, duplicates q-quiz1a-seaanchor-substitute), Q3 (24-hour water/food rule, duplicates the premise of q-quiz1a-first-water-liferaft), Q10 (3 lifeboatmen for 50 persons, duplicates q-vinci-coi-lifeboatmen-4160's 41-60 band), Q13 (lifeboats lowered every 3 months, duplicates q-cfr-lifeboat-lowered-quarterly word-for-word). Q4's core hatchet-count/location fact also duplicates live q-quiz1a-bow-stern-hatchet and was not redrafted; only its new 'near the releasing gear' placement detail was drafted as a distinct question.

questions.prompt: Your vessel has 3 lifeboats on each side, numbered by standard convention. Where is #4 lifeboat located relative to the other boats on its side?

choices.text: On the port side, aft of #2 and forward of #6
On the starboard side, aft of #2 and forward of #6
choices.misconception: Even-numbered lifeboats are on the port side by convention, not starboard.

choices.text: On the port side, forward of #2
choices.misconception: Reverses the fore-to-aft ascending order -- #2 is the forward-most boat on the port side, not #4.

choices.text: It could be on either side, depending on the vessel
choices.misconception: Lifeboat numbering follows a standardized, not vessel-specific, convention.

hints: Even numbers are on the port side, ascending from forward to aft -- so with 3 boats per side numbered #2, #4, #6, where does #4 fall?
questions.explanation: By standard convention, port-side lifeboats are even-numbered, ascending from forward to aft. With 3 boats per side (#2, #4, #6), #4 sits on the port side, aft of #2 and forward of #6.
questions.sourceReference: Vinci source batch 2026-07-08-quiz-3, image 2026-07-07_064497-2.jpg (Review Quiz #3, question 8 of 14); see src/content/imports/vinci-2026-07-08-quiz-3-source-intake.json. CONFIDENCE NOTE: the student's answer clearly states 'portside of the vessel' and 'forward of lifeboat #6'; the 'aft of #2' portion was reconstructed from the already-live standard numbering convention (q-ch2-numbering-official-vh) rather than directly read from the handwriting, since the connecting word linking to the inserted 'lifeboat #2' note was not clearly legible. Recommended needs-review rather than quiz-derived-study for this reason, even though it's a worked-example application of an already-confirmed live rule rather than a new fact.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is NOT typically something a ship's Station Bill lists?
hints: A Station Bill is about emergency preparedness and duties, not cargo logistics.
questions.explanation: A Station Bill lists emergency-related items -- alarms, signals, and instructions; individual crew members' emergency duties; and the specific alarm signals used (e.g., the fire alarm). A cargo manifest and stowage plan is a separate document, unrelated to emergency muster/duty assignments.
questions.sourceReference: Vinci source batch 2026-07-08-quiz-3, image 2026-07-07_064497-2.jpg (Review Quiz #3, question 7 of 14); see src/content/imports/vinci-2026-07-08-quiz-3-source-intake.json. Note: the live q-ch1-vocab-stationbill-easy already tests the bare definition of a Station Bill; this question tests the same general fact from an enumerated-list/elimination angle using this student's own itemization (emergency alarms/signals/instructions, fire alarm, emergency duties, alarm signals). Given the substantial overlap with already-live content and the low amount of genuinely new information, recommended needs-review / low promotion priority rather than a front-line status, per the same reasoning already applied to other review-variant content in this project (e.g. q-vinci-limber-holes).

promotionHistory.note: Promotion of the 7 questions marked 'ready for promotion now' in vinci-2026-07-08-quiz-3-promotion-review.md. The remaining 2 questions stay in this file, inactive: q-quiz3-lifeboat4-location-scenario (needs a legibility recheck on a reconstructed detail) and q-quiz3-stationbill-itemslist (low-value near-duplicate of live q-ch1-vocab-stationbill-easy).
